entry.title: MAD-HTLC
entry.summary: The recent discourse concerning the MAD-HTLC paper highlights some uncertainties regarding the specifics of collateral size, noted as \( v^{col} \). The inquiry specifically seeks clarification on whether the paper addresses the amount of collateral required in such agreements. It is assumed that the collateral \( v^{col} \) should at least match the deposit size \( v^{dep} \), to prevent any potential for an HTLC bribery attack where one party might disproportionately threaten the other with minimal personal risk. This assumption is based on the principle that both parties involved should have equal stakes at risk to maintain balance and fairness in the transaction. The concern is that without equivalent values at risk, the integrity of the transaction could be compromised, leading to possible security vulnerabilities.
